prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week is an annual fashion industry event held in Sydney, Australia. The event is a part of Australian Fashion Week and has taken place at Carriageworks for two consecutive years. The 2014 event was held from 6–10 April. The event focuses on providing Australian designers a platform to connect with a global audience of fashion buyers, press and consumers.The industry-only event showcased over 70 designers, including 25 designers from Sydney, four from Melbourne, four from Perth, two from Brisbane and one from Adelaide. There was also one designer from Bali and China. The 2014 designer showcase featured mostly start-up labels fresh from fashion school. Lan Yu became the first Chinese designer to showcase at Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week Australia. In 2013, Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week Sydney generated an
B. The Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week is a yearly event that takes place at Carriageworks in Sydney, Australia. The event is organized by the fashion industry and is part of Australian Fashion Week. The 2014 event was held from 6-10 April and aimed to connect Australian designers with a global audience of fashion buyers, press, and consumers. The event featured over 70 designers, including 25 from Sydney, four from Melbourne, four from Perth, two from Brisbane, and one from Adelaide. The 2014 designer showcase showcased mostly start-up labels from China.
C. Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week, an annual fixture in the fashion calendar, is staged in Sydney, Australia. This event is a component of Australian Fashion Week and has been hosted at Carriageworks for two years in a row. The 2014 iteration of the event occurred between April 6th and April 10th. Its primary objective is to offer Australian designers a venue to engage with an international audience comprising fashion buyers, members of the press, and consumers.

This exclusive industry gathering presented the collections of more than 70 designers. Among them were 25 designers hailing from Sydney, four from Melbourne, four from Perth, two from Brisbane, and one from Adelaide. Additionally, there was one designer representing Bali and another from China. The designer presentations in 2014 predominantly highlighted emerging brands directly out of fashion academies. Notably, Lan Yu achieved the distinction of being the inaugural Chinese designer to present at Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week Australia.
